steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: “emulsifiers such as stearyl alcohol ______ to blend oil and water into homogeneous formulations.” The blank is the main verb of the sentence. It must be a finite verb that agrees in number with the plural subject emulsifiers and fits the present-tense context.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘employed’: A past participle used alone cannot serve as a finite verb; it leaves the sentence without a working predicate. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘being employed’: A present participle phrase cannot stand alone as a finite verb either; this would produce a fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘are employed’: A plural present-tense passive verb that agrees with the plural subject emulsifiers and completes the sentence grammatically: “emulsifiers … are employed to blend …” ✓
  • Choice D — ‘that they employ’: Inserting a relative clause here creates a dangling construction with no clear antecedent for they and leaves the main clause without a predicate. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
